long_description: Adapter Kind: Adapter Instance: VMWARE - parm001-vc.m001.parpr.dir - vSAN - Collecting - Data receiving VMWARE - parm001-vc.m001.parpr.dir - Collecting - Data receiving vRealizeOpsMgrAPI (vRealize Operations Collector-vrops-master) - Collecting - Data receiving Alert Description: One or more guest file systems of the virtual machine are running out of disk space. Check the symptom details to identify the affected guest file system Alert ID: 4aac2ddc-01f6-4216-bb14-e085b9440787 Alert Impact: health Alert Name: One or more virtual machine guest file systems are running out of disk space - IBM Alert Subtype: Capacity Alert Type: Virtualization/Hypervisor Cancel Date: Unavailable Control State: Open Criticality: warning Host Name: vrops-data02.parpr.dir Alert Link: https://172.22.2.53/vcf-operations/ui/inventory;mode=hierarchy;resourceId=ae7d92cb-396f-4ced-bf9d-349f4a774742;tab=alerts-and-symptoms;subTab=alerts;entityId=4aac2ddc-01f6-4216-bb14-e085b9440787 Notification Rule Description: Notification Rule Name: PD: One or more virtual machine guest file systems are running out of disk space Object Efficiency State: 1.0 Object Health State: 2.0 Object Risk State: 1.0 Product: ['N/A', 'N/A', 'N/A'] Recommendations: Runbook: https://pages.github.ibm.com/cloudlab/internal-docs/vrops_sev1_alerts.html#one-or-more-guest-filesystem-is-running-out-of-disk-space | Add a new virtual hard disk or expand the existing disk of the virtual machine. You must first remove all snapshots before expanding an existing disk. After completing this, use a guest OS specific procedure to expand the file system on the new or expanded disk. Runbook URL: https://pages.github.ibm.com/cloudlab/internal-docs/vrops_sev1_alerts.html#one-or-more-guest-filesystem-is-running-out-of-disk-space Resource ID: ae7d92cb-396f-4ced-bf9d-349f4a774742 Resource Kind: VirtualMachine Resource Name: p5w006s05vrl035 Status: Active Symptoms: Symptom Set - self Symptoms Guest file system space usage at warning level | p5w006s05vrl035 | ae7d92cb-396f-4ced-bf9d-349f4a774742 guestfilesystem:/|percentage : 91.215 > 90.0 Recommendations: Runbook: https://pages.github.ibm.com/cloudlab/internal-docs/vrops_sev1_alerts.html#one-or-more-guest-filesystem-is-running-out-of-disk-space | Add a new virtual hard disk or expand the existing disk of the virtual machine. You must first remove all snapshots before expanding an existing disk. After completing this, use a guest OS specific procedure to expand the file system on the new or expanded disk.
